creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
videos.summary: Matt Pocock introduces a "Skill Checklist Framework" for building high-quality AI agent skills. The framework addresses "skill hell" through four components: Trigger (user-invoked vs model-invoked decisions), Structure (organizing steps and references), Steering (using leading words to guide agent behavior), and Pruning (removing obsolete material and no-ops).

videos.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
videos.summary: RL Nabors argues that most applications unnecessarily default to frontier models at high cost in latency, energy, and money when small local models would suffice. The talk provides vocabulary for model evaluation (capability evals, golden datasets, LLM-as-judge) and a framework for deciding when to use small local models versus frontier models, supported by production-trace-based eval examples.

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together form a more powerful and reliable AI architecture than skills-based approaches. This composition pattern enables smaller models to perform real work and moves AI from internal tools to customer-facing products.

videos.title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
videos.summary: Balázs Horváth argues that AI code generation has shifted the bottleneck from implementation to stakeholder management and requirements elicitation. He presents a framework using story mapping and value assessment to distinguish production agents from demos, grounded in a VisualLabs hackathon where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned due to poor problem framing rather than technical limitations.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model choice is only 10% of success while the surrounding harness of context, tools, and verification determines 90%. The video covers the evolution from vibe coding to structured agentic engineering, the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles, and the economic implications of this approach.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a cross-tool memory layer that persists AI agent context across different LLM providers. It stores encrypted memories owned by the user, allowing different agents to access shared context regardless of which platform they run on.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. The reviewer describes it as impressive for its price and size.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Loop engineering enables agents to prompt themselves iteratively rather than relying on manual prompting. An orchestrator agent spins up worker agents in parallel to execute tasks, with all runs persisted to a database for observability and debugging. This approach trades computational cost for reliability in agentic systems.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenClaw Creator's new project involving Crabbox and a codebase harness. The video links to a GitHub repository with skills setup, an AI Builder Club course on loop engineering, and Superdesign.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason presents Loop Engineer, a tool for building AI agents, with a GitHub repository, workshop access, and integration with Superdesign. The video covers setup instructions and practical implementation guidance.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video covers how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving companies using autonomous feedback loops. It demonstrates practical use cases like autonomous ad optimization and SEO, emphasizing the role of memory and tool integration in enabling these systems.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and autonomous coding workflows, presenting recent developments in agentic coding systems. The video covers setup, prompting strategies, and long-horizon task execution capabilities within coding frameworks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains OpenAI's announcement of GPT 5.6 and discusses how government regulation is maturing the AI industry. The video compares GPT 5.6 with Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models, noting both face regulatory pauses, and highlights OpenAI's consolidation of models into three tiers: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: A video tutorial explaining how generative pretrained transformers work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video demonstrates that GPT's basic mechanics are accessible to learn, using the example of models like Gemini, Opus, Kimi, Nemotron, and Qwen that rely on transformer architecture.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Educational video explaining Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model as a pedagogical stepping stone toward GPT. Covers tokenization, embeddings, cross-entropy loss, softmax, and optimizer training to demonstrate core LLM mechanics.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer, Mixture of Experts (latent MoE), and multi-token prediction architectures.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Containers on Amazon ECS with Mama J
videos.summary: AWS Developers video explaining containerization, Docker basics, and Amazon ECS orchestration. Covers the problem containers solve, VM vs container differences, ECS as an orchestrator, and Fargate vs EC2 deployment options with a shopping app example.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents a system design interview framework with seven sequential steps: requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level component design, API specification, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The approach emphasizes understanding the problem and business requirements before choosing technology.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling enables AI agents to request external resources and actions rather than generating responses from training data alone. The technique involves agents invoking tools via schema-based requests, framework execution, and loop iteration. System prompts determine when agents use tools appropriately, distinguishing agents from stateless chatbots.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with up-to-date documentation, best practices, and guardrails for AWS service integration. It improves agent success rates and reduces wasted effort by providing tested procedures instead of relying on outdated general knowledge.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Google engineers use code generation tools and compares OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities. The video explores code generation adoption trends at Google across 2024-2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source, discussing its implications for open source maintainers and the ecosystem. The video frames the topic as a provocative debate about whether Malus represents an existential threat to open source.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: AI Hackers Are Targeting Ordinary People Now, Not Just Big Tech | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
videos.summary: Damien Lewke, founder of Nebulock, argues that AI has drastically lowered the barrier to entry for cyberattacks, enabling small teams with a GPU to execute complex attacks that previously required elite specialists. He advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture and outlines behavioral signals that indicate attackers are already inside networks.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, co-founder and CEO of Gong, argues that software moats depend on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that fears of AI destroying SaaS companies are exaggerated, and that the best AI products create devoted customers.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT Moment" is coming for robotics but the focus on humanoid form factors by companies like 1X and Unitree may be limiting. She contends that future robot design should prioritize human interaction and delight over utility alone, with billions of robots expected to enter everyday life.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how the startup competes against much larger enterprise software incumbents by building a complete platform from day one rather than a single feature. She explains Pigment's growth strategy centered on gaining trust through high-profile customer endorsements, why legacy spreadsheet-based planning fails at scale, and her hiring philosophy focused on raising organizational standards.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Anthropic vs. Microsoft: The AI Agent Integration Battle! #shorts
videos.summary: Anthropic seeks direct Teams integration while Microsoft pursues the agent market. The piece questions whether Microsoft's ecosystem strategy will dominate in direct competition.

videos.title: Why China's AI Models Are Taking Over: CHEAPER & FASTER! #shorts
videos.summary: Companies are increasingly adopting Chinese AI models due to cost savings and data sovereignty assurances. The commentary presents this as a market shift driven by economic and regulatory advantages over established providers.

videos.title: AI Digital Employee? Claude Tagging & The AI Market Revealed! #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses AI assistants like Claude being integrated into workplace collaboration platforms like Slack and Microsoft Teams as digital employees. It covers the evolving AI market and introduces the concept of loop engineering as an innovative approach to AI-assisted work.

videos.title: AI Frontier vs Open Source: The Gap is CLOSING FAST! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that the performance gap between open-source and frontier AI models is closing rapidly, with the pace of innovation accelerating from annual to monthly intervals. This reflects a shift in the competitive landscape of AI development.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
